title: Age Structure Diagram Explorer
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive population pyramid explorer comparing demographics of 10 countries with animated transitions and compare mode

learningObjectives: Interpret age structure diagrams to predict future population trends for different countries
Classify population pyramids as expansive, stationary, or constrictive
Compare demographic profiles across countries at different stages of the demographic transition
Relate total fertility rate and median age to pyramid shape
prerequisites: Understanding of population growth concepts (birth rate, death rate)
Basic knowledge of the demographic transition model

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Comparative visual analysis with animated transitions
keyQuestions: How does pyramid shape predict a country's future population growth or decline?
What is the relationship between total fertility rate and pyramid shape?
Why does China's pyramid look so different from other countries with similar GDP?
How does the dependency ratio affect a nation's economic capacity?
What does the UAE's pyramid reveal about labor migration patterns?
commonMisconceptions: A large population always means rapid growth (growth rate depends on age structure, not just size)
Population pyramids are static snapshots (they represent dynamic processes that change over decades)
All developing countries have expansive pyramids (many are transitioning, like India and Brazil)
assessmentOpportunities: Have students predict 2050 population trends from pyramid shape alone, then check against projections
Ask students to classify unlabeled pyramids by demographic stage
Compare two countries and explain the socioeconomic implications of their different age structures
